title: Bind every new Grabowski checkout to owner, TTL and closure
goal: Extend the existing safe checkout lifecycle so newly created operator worktrees require owner, purpose, source task or PR, retention and artifact class, and so terminal clean checkouts become archive-and-cleanup candidates after a grace period.

approval.note: Source changes and read-only verification are authorized. Runtime deletion or cleanup effects require a fresh exact-target plan and post-readback.

acceptance.assertion: Worktree creation fails closed when owner, purpose, retention and source binding are absent.
Per-repository active and completed-retained checkout limits are enforced without deleting existing foreign checkouts.
Task or PR completion can produce a recovery archive and cleanup dry-run candidate after a 24-hour grace period.
Dirty state, active tasks, processes, resource leases and main worktrees remain hard blockers.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit
does_not_establish: permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards
metadata.readiness_basis: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T002 verified by merged Heim-PC PR #47, green CI, byte-identical merge readback, live managed-build receipt and installed operator projection.
validation: 1,768 repository tests passed with one expected skip on the exact published head.
Access policy, tool-surface budget, operator-context and publication-profile generators, runtime locks, deploy-tooling reproducibility and secret scan passed.
GitHub validate checks for Python 3.10 and 3.12 passed on the exact reviewed head.
Post-merge focused tests proved active-limit rejection without deletion, fresh-archive grace rejection and two-stage Agent Workspace cleanup.

verified_acceptance.creation-contract: The deployed worktree-ensure grip rejected a request missing purpose, retention_until_unix, source_kind, source_id and artifact_class during preflight before Git mutation.
verified_acceptance.limits: Managed physical active and completed-retained lifecycle rows are bounded per Git common directory; expiry alone does not bypass the limit, and the focused merge-commit test proved rejection without deleting an existing checkout.
verified_acceptance.closure: Archive and lifecycle transition are one SQLite transaction; a fresh archive remains retained for at least 24 hours and requires a new hash-bound cleanup plan after the grace period.
verified_acceptance.safety: Main worktree, dirty state, active tasks, processes, relevant leases, ownership, retention and identity drift remain hard blockers; no foreign checkout was removed during verification.

completion.outcome: New managed checkouts are identity-, owner-, source- and retention-bound before mutation; growth is bounded and cleanup remains archive-, grace-, plan- and blocker-guarded.
